description: Unified Detail & Staging Panel — Replace stacked DetailPanel + StatusPanel with a single context-aware right panel that switches between staging and commit details based on graph selection. Add a virtual WIP row to the commit graph representing uncommitted changes.

userStories.title: WIP row data model and status integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the CommitGraph component to maintain a WIP (Work In Progress) data model that tracks uncommitted working tree changes, so that later stories can render it as a virtual row.
acceptanceCriteria: CommitGraph maintains a `wipStatus` state: { staged: number, unstaged: number, untracked: number } | null
wipStatus is loaded on initial mount by calling window.electronAPI.git.getStatus(repoPath) and counting the arrays
wipStatus updates when repo:changed events fire (via the existing onRepoChanged listener)
wipStatus is null when all counts are zero (clean working tree)
A new `isWipSelected` state (boolean, default false) tracks whether the WIP row is selected
When isWipSelected is true, onCommitSelect is called with null (no commit detail to show — the parent will use this to switch to staging mode)
Export a new WipStatus type from CommitGraph.tsx: { staged: number, unstaged: number, untracked: number }
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Don't render the WIP row yet — just set up the data model and selection state. Use the existing getStatus IPC call. The status data shape from the IPC is { branch, upstream, ahead, behind, staged: FileStatus[], unstaged: FileStatus[], untracked: FileStatus[] } — count the array lengths.

userStories.title: Render WIP row in commit graph
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a WIP row at the top of the commit graph when I have uncommitted changes, so that I can click it to access the staging area.
acceptanceCriteria: When wipStatus is non-null, a WIP row renders at the top of the commit graph (index 0 in react-window)
All real commit indices shift by 1 when the WIP row is visible (itemCount = commits.length + 1 when wipStatus is present, commits.length otherwise)
The WIP row uses a dotted/dashed circle node on lane 0 (HEAD lane) with a dashed line connecting to the HEAD commit below
The WIP row message column shows 'Working Tree Changes'
The WIP row shows a change summary in the author column area: e.g. '2 staged, 5 unstaged' or '3 files changed'
The WIP row has no hash, no date column content
Clicking the WIP row selects it (highlighted with the selected row style) and sets isWipSelected to true
Clicking a real commit row sets isWipSelected to false and works as before
The WIP row is hidden when wipStatus is null (clean working tree)
All existing click handlers, context menus, keyboard navigation, and scroll-to-commit work correctly with the index offset
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: The WIP row is NOT a real GraphNode — create a special rendering path in the row component. Check `index === 0 && wipStatus` to render the WIP row, otherwise render commits[index - wipOffset]. wipOffset = wipStatus ? 1 : 0. Update handleRowClick, handleRowContextMenu, keyboard nav (up/down), handleRowsRendered, selectedIndex, headInfo, and the scroll-to-commit listener to account for the offset. The SVG graph renderer also needs to handle the WIP node — draw a dashed circle at lane 0, row 0, with a dashed line to the HEAD commit at row 1.

userStories.title: Unified right panel — mode switching in AppLayout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the right panel to show the staging area when WIP is selected and commit details when a commit is selected, so that I have one clean panel instead of two cramped stacked panels.
acceptanceCriteria: AppLayout renders a single panel in the right column that switches between StatusPanel and DetailPanel based on whether WIP or a commit is selected
When selectedCommit is null (WIP selected or nothing selected): StatusPanel is shown, DetailPanel is hidden
When selectedCommit is non-null (real commit selected): DetailPanel is shown, StatusPanel is hidden

CRITICAL: Both StatusPanel and DetailPanel must remain mounted at all times — use CSS display:none to hide the inactive one, do NOT conditionally unmount. This preserves commit form state (subject, body, amend) when switching between modes.
The old stacked layout (DetailPanel on top, StatusPanel on bottom, drag handle between them) is removed
The detailStagingSplit drag handle is removed
Both 'right' and 'bottom' panel position variants use the unified panel
The panel has no collapse toggle in detail mode. In staging mode, individual sections (unstaged/staged) are collapsible (handled by StatusPanel internally, see US-UP-004)
On initial repo load: if there are working tree changes, WIP is auto-selected (staging visible). If clean, HEAD is auto-selected (detail visible)
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: The key implementation pattern: render both <StatusPanel style={{display: isWipOrNothingSelected ? undefined : 'none'}} /> and <DetailPanel style={{display: isWipOrNothingSelected ? 'none' : undefined}} /> inside a single container div. This keeps both mounted for state preservation. Remove the isDraggingDetailSplit state, handleDetailSplitDragStart, detailStagingSplit from useLayoutState, and the drag handle div between the two panels. The selectedCommit state in AppLayout already tracks this — when CommitGraph calls onCommitSelect(null) for WIP selection, selectedCommit becomes null, which switches to staging mode.

userStories.title: Staging panel — collapsible Unstaged and Staged sections
userStories.description: As a user, I want to individually collapse the Unstaged Changes and Staged Changes sections in the staging panel like GitKraken, so that I can focus on one section at a time.
acceptanceCriteria: The staging panel (StatusPanel) has two distinct sections: 'Unstaged Changes' header with file count badge and collapse chevron, and 'Staged Changes' header with file count badge and collapse chevron
Clicking a section header toggles its collapsed/expanded state
When collapsed, only the header row is visible (chevron + title + file count + Stage All/Unstage All button)
The commit form (subject input, body textarea, commit button, amend/signoff/gpg checkboxes) is always visible below the Staged Changes section — it is never collapsible
Collapse state persists when switching between WIP and commit detail modes (since StatusPanel stays mounted)
Stage All button is in the Unstaged section header, Unstage All is in the Staged section header
File click, right-click context menu, and drag-and-drop between sections all still work
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: StatusPanel already has a file list rendering system. The change is to split the single file list into two sections with independent collapse state. Use useState<boolean> for unstagedCollapsed and stagedCollapsed (default false = expanded). The existing 'Stage All' and 'Unstage All' buttons can move into the section headers. Keep the existing onFileSelect callback for routing diffs to the center panel.

userStories.title: Layout state cleanup and dead code removal
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove unused layout state fields and dead code from the old stacked panel layout, so that the codebase stays clean.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove detailStagingSplit field from useLayoutState (or leave as deprecated no-op if removing causes migration issues with stored localStorage data)
Remove detailPanelCollapsed field and toggleDetailPanelCollapse from useLayoutState
Remove stagingCollapsed field and toggleStagingCollapse from useLayoutState if no longer used (the new collapse is per-section inside StatusPanel)
Remove the isDraggingDetailSplit state, handleDetailSplitDragStart, and handleDetailSplitDoubleClick from AppLayout
Remove any leftover references to the old stacked panel layout in AppLayout, MainContent, or other files
Verify no console warnings about unused variables or imports
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Be careful with localStorage migration — if the stored layout state has detailStagingSplit and the code tries to read it, it should gracefully handle the missing field. The simplest approach: keep the field in the interface but stop reading/writing it, then remove it in a future version. Or add a migration that strips it.

userStories.title: Working tree diffs in center panel — verify and fix
userStories.description: As a user, I want clicking a file in the staging area to open its diff in the center panel, so that I get a full-width view of my changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a staged or unstaged file in the staging panel opens its diff in the center panel (replacing the commit graph)
The center panel header shows the file path and indicates it's a working tree diff (not a commit diff)
Clicking 'Back to Graph' returns to the commit graph with the WIP row still selected
The diff view mode toggle (Diff / Full / File) works for working tree diffs
Untracked files show in File view (no diff available for new files)
After committing changes, the center panel returns to the graph view and the WIP row updates or disappears if the tree is now clean
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: This functionality mostly exists already via the workingTreeFile prop system added in the staging diff overhaul. This story is primarily about verifying it still works after the panel refactor and fixing any broken connections. The key flow: StatusPanel calls onFileSelect → AppLayout sets workingTreeFile → RepoView shows the diff in center panel. Make sure the 'Back to Graph' button clears workingTreeFile AND keeps the WIP row selected.
